hawkish_phrases: appropriately forceful or persistent monetary policy action in response to large, sustained deviations of inflation from the target in either direction
the recent high-inflation period had shown that a forceful reaction was also needed when inflation rose above target
the Governing Council had been forced to raise rates very sharply to compensate for a delay in starting the tightening cycle
it would have been desirable to withdraw accommodation earlier and raise rates more gradually
the bar for large-scale QE needed to be higher in future
dovish_phrases: the economy had entered a period where the risks to medium-term inflation are two-sided
uncertainty that was both of a different nature and greater than before
importance of avoiding deviations becoming entrenched through de-anchored inflation expectations in either direction
the existence of an effective lower bound on policy rates
summary: The Governing Council unanimously approved an updated monetary policy strategy statement with no policy decision taken, emphasizing a genuinely two-sided approach to inflation risks in an environment of high uncertainty, with balanced language regarding forceful responses to deviations in either direction.
